title: sidalcea neomexicana subsp. diehlii
intro: Sidalcea neomexicana subsp. diehlii, also known as Diehl's checkerbloom, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the southwestern United States. It is a member of the mallow family and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and providing antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. Sidalcea neomexicana subsp. diehlii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be done under the guidance of a medical professional due to its potential side effects and interactions.
